sources.note: Overhead Wildlife Locator (OWL): Benchmarking Weakly Supervised Learning for Aerial Wildlife Surveys, Microsoft AI for Good Lab et al. Source for the weakly-supervised point-label method, the 7x-faster / 3x-cheaper labeling claim, F1 = 0.965 and +3.1% signed count error on ADF&G's 2022 Central Arctic Caribou census, OWL-D beating HerdNet (0.934 vs 0.840 AP) on Delplanque, and the 'performance is regime-dependent' caveat (OWL-D degrades on the densest herds).
Ships the OWL model family (OWL-C / OWL-T / OWL-D) and a Caribou-OWL-C weight; confirms the training, evaluation, and inference stack described in the paper.

sources.outlet: OWL paper (HTML full text) — affiliations + quotes
sources.note: Verifies the on-screen numbers verbatim and the author affiliations: Microsoft AI for Good Lab-led, with Nathan J. Pamperin (Alaska Department of Fish and Game) as the ADF&G co-author and ADF&G's 2022 census data. Not a Microsoft/ADF&G co-development beyond that.
